skill.name: applying-brand-guidelines
skill.description: Applies corporate brand standards to document configurations and checks content for brand compliance.
skill.summary: Apply colors, fonts, layouts, tone rules, and validation checks for branded business documents.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: The high and critical static findings are false positives caused by brand color hex codes, sample labels, and markdown code fences. No prompt injection, shell execution, network access, credential handling, or malicious intent was found. The remaining low risk is local Python script behavior and a helper that reads a caller-supplied JSON guidelines file.

low_findings.title: Static color and sample text alerts are false positives
low_findings.description: Static findings for weak cryptography and Windows SAM data refer to brand color hex codes, documentation wording, and the SAMPLE watermark label. These lines do not implement cryptography, access system files, or process credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visible as color notation, document labels, or brand documentation. I found no cryptographic API calls, Windows SAM path usage, or credential access nearby.

low_findings.title: Markdown examples misidentified as command execution
low_findings.description: Backtick alerts in the reference and skill files are markdown code fences, sample templates, file names, and example document names. They are not Ruby shell backticks or executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are markdown documentation and list entries. There is no shell invocation syntax in Python source and no instruction to execute these snippets.

low_findings.title: Environment and reconnaissance alerts are false positives
low_findings.description: The environment access alert is a dictionary title lookup, and system reconnaissance alerts refer to brand text, JSON error documentation, or font names. No environment variables, host inventory, or system probing were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are content lookup, style guidance, and exception documentation. Searches found no process environment access or reconnaissance commands.

low_findings.title: Local guidelines file loader
low_findings.description: The validation helper can read a caller-supplied JSON file containing brand guidelines. This is legitimate functionality, but users should only pass trusted guideline files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read is explicit and limited to JSON loading for guidelines. I found no network transmission or hidden exfiltration path.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when documents use inconsistent colors, fonts, tone, and layout rules. This skill provides reusable brand standards, formatting helpers, and validation checks for business documents.
actual_capabilities: Defines Acme Corporation colors, fonts, tone rules, logo usage, and document layout standards.
Applies branded configuration settings for Excel, PowerPoint, and PDF document types.
Validates color usage, font usage, tone keywords, prohibited words, and brand name capitalization.
Returns compliance scores, violations, warnings, and practical suggestions for content revisions.
Provides chart palettes, watermark settings, and number formatting rules aligned to the brand.
limitations: Uses example Acme Corporation standards until users replace them with their own brand rules.
Returns formatting configurations and validation reports, not finished document files.
Does not verify actual logo image quality, placement, or accessibility in rendered documents.
Reads local guideline JSON only when a caller provides a file path to the loader.

use_cases.title: Standardize sales presentations
use_cases.description: Apply approved colors, title layouts, chart palettes, and footer rules before sharing client presentations.

use_cases.title: Review marketing drafts
use_cases.description: Check campaign copy for brand tone, prohibited wording, color choices, and correct brand name usage.

use_cases.title: Prepare executive reports
use_cases.description: Use consistent PDF headers, footers, typography, tables, watermarks, and number formats for leadership documents.

prompt_templates.title: Apply basic branding
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the brand guidelines to this document outline. Use the approved colors, fonts, headings, and tone rules.

prompt_templates.title: Validate existing content
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for review before publishing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this draft for brand compliance. List violations, warnings, and suggested fixes for color, font, tone, and brand name usage.

prompt_templates.title: Create document formatting plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for multiple output formats.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a formatting plan for Excel, PowerPoint, and PDF versions of this report using the brand standards.

prompt_templates.title: Customize brand rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request to adapt the example standards.
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace the example Acme standards with these organization rules, then explain how the validation checks should change.

output_examples.input: A quarterly report uses generic blue, mixed fonts, and an informal opening paragraph.
output_examples.output: The review identifies unapproved color use, inconsistent typography, and tone issues. It recommends Acme Blue, Segoe UI, and professional opening language.

output_examples.input: A PowerPoint deck needs branded slide structure and chart styling.
output_examples.output: The result describes a title slide, content slide, footer treatment, chart palette, logo placement, and approved spacing rules.

output_examples.input: A finance spreadsheet needs brand styling for headers, rows, borders, and numbers.
output_examples.output: The result specifies branded header colors, alternating row treatment, neutral borders, right-aligned numbers, and approved currency formatting.

best_practices: Replace the example company details with approved standards from your brand team before production use.
Validate drafts before final export so formatting and tone issues are easy to correct.
Keep accessibility requirements with the brand rules, including contrast ratios and minimum font sizes.
anti_patterns: Do not treat example Acme Corporation values as your own organization standards.
Do not rely on visual review alone when documents contain many colors, fonts, or reused templates.
Do not pass untrusted local files to the guideline JSON loader.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with my own brand?
faq.answer: Yes. Replace the example colors, fonts, tone keywords, company name, and prohibited words with your approved standards.

faq.question: Does this skill create finished documents?
faq.answer: No. It provides formatting configurations, validation checks, and guidance that can be applied during document creation.

faq.question: Which document types are covered?
faq.answer: The helper code includes Excel, PowerPoint, and PDF configuration patterns, plus general content validation.

faq.question: Does it check accessibility?
faq.answer: The reference includes contrast and font size standards. The validator mainly checks colors, fonts, tone, and brand name usage.

faq.question: Is the included company real?
faq.answer: No. Acme Corporation is example content used to show how brand rules can be structured.

faq.question: Does this skill send data outside my environment?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files contain no network calls or credential transmission logic.
